attempts.test_code:
import threading
import pytest
from source import sqrt


def test_sqrt_converges_to_actual_square_root():
    """Buggy check abs(x - approx) measures distance from x itself; once approx
    converges to sqrt(x) the residual stabilises at abs(x - sqrt(x)) ≈ 0.586
    for x=2, so the loop never terminates.  The fixed check abs(x - approx**2)
    reaches zero as approx → sqrt(x) and exits correctly."""
    result = [None]

    def run():
        result[0] = sqrt(2, 0.01)

    t = threading.Thread(target=run, daemon=True)
    t.start()
    t.join(timeout=3)

    assert not t.is_alive(), (
        "sqrt(2, 0.01) did not terminate within 3 s — "
        "wrong convergence check abs(x - approx) causes an infinite loop "
        "because approx converges to sqrt(x), not to x"
    )

    assert abs(result[0] - 1.41421356) < 0.01, (
        f"Expected sqrt(2, 0.01) ≈ 1.414 (within epsilon=0.01), got {result[0]}"
    )
